crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Our West Asia stress test of 34 sectors Prolonged conflict to shave ~200 bps off corporate profitability
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The protracted conflict in West Asia has been goading domestic companies to realign supply chains, navigate pricing issues, manage higher fuel and freight costs, and contend with a depreciating rupee.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Capital goods players' double-digit growth momentum to continue
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Sustained government spending, steady capacity expansion in power, mining, oil and gas, metal and auto-linked sectors, and increasing opportunities from emerging segments such as data centres and electric vehicle (EV) infrastructure will ensure a revenue growth of 12-14% for the capital goods indust

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Duty hike spells decadal-low volume for gold jewellery retailers
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The Indian organised gold jewellery retail sector, comprising jewellery, coins and bars, is expected to see sales volume decline a further 13-15% on-year this fiscal, after an 8% contraction last fiscal, due to high prices of gold and recent policy measures to curb imports of the metal.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Organised FMCG players' steady 8-10% revenue growth to be price-led
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The organised fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) sector is expected to post 8–10% revenue growth this fiscal, a tad higher than ~8% witnessed in fiscal 2026. The uptick in growth will be driven by a sharp 6–7% increase in realisations as players partially pass on the increase in prices of crude-linke

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Road InvITs AUM to grow 30% this fiscal led by asset monetisation surge
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Infrastructure investment trusts (InvITs) have emerged as a key investment vehicle for enabling monetisation of infrastructure assets by private as well as public sector entities. Specifically, road sector InvITs are poised to see their assets under management (AUM) rise to ~Rs 3.9 lakh crore by Mar

crisil-research.title: Time to overhaul branch expansion models
crisil-research.description: The process typically begins with gathering credit data to identify geographies with credit-active households, commercial density and visible signs of economic activity.

crisil-research.title: The CCAR drive
crisil-research.description: Regional banks in the United States nearing $100 billion in total consolidated assets should already be preparing for the Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review (CCAR), rather than waiting for the regulatory clock to start ticking.

crisil-research.title: The increasing complexity of underwriting in private banking
crisil-research.description: US regional banks are steadily expanding their private banking capabilities, making deliberate, expensive bets on the segment as the next engine of profitable growth. And at the heart of that bet lies a critical capability: to underwrite complex, bespoke credit for high and ultra-high net worth (HNW

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: Crude jolt
crisil-research.description: A 34.7% on-year surge in petroleum exports helped India’s goods exports rise 13.8% on-year in April to $43.6 billion (vs a 7.4% contraction in March). Core1 exports were resilient, rising 10.4% on-year to $31.6 billion, albeit on a low base.

crisil-research.title: Quickonomics: Oil's not well
crisil-research.description: India’s crude oil trade deficit has been under the pump historically because of having to meet over 85% of its annual requirement from imports.

axis-direct-research.title: Daily Fundamental View:May 26, 2026
axis-direct-research.description: Asian markets are trading lower as investors remain cautious amid ongoing geopolitical tensions. The Hang Seng, Shanghai, and Nikkei were down by 0.58%, 0.62%, and 0.63%, respectively.

axis-direct-research.title: Result Update:NTPC
axis-direct-research.description: We maintain our BUY rating on the stock.

axis-direct-research.title: Result Update:Minda Corp
axis-direct-research.description: We recommend a BUY on the stock.

axis-direct-research.title: Result Update:LG Electronics
axis-direct-research.description: We maintain our BUY rating on the stock, with a target price of Rs 1,815/share, implying an upside of 22% from the CMP.

axis-direct-research.title: Result Update:Hindalco Inds.
axis-direct-research.description: We revise our rating on the stock from HOLD to BUY.

axis-direct-research.title: Result Update:Eicher Motors
axis-direct-research.description: We maintain a BUY rating on the stock, driven by strong volume momentum across Royal Enfield and VECV, supported by favourable market conditions.

axis-direct-research.title: Result Update:Prestige Estates
axis-direct-research.description: We maintain our BUY recommendation on the stock and continue to value the company using a DCF-based valuation to arrive at a TP of Rs 1,830/share, implying a 32% upside from the CMP.

axis-direct-research.title: Daily Fundamental View:May 25, 2026
axis-direct-research.description: Asian markets are trading higher as hopes of the Strait of Hormuz reopening soon eased oil prices, thereby boosting investor sentiment. The Shanghai Composite and Nikkei indices were trading positively by 0.47% and 3.1%, respectively, while the Hang Seng Index remained closed for the day.

axis-direct-research.title: Result Update:Colgate-Palmoliv
axis-direct-research.description: With an upside of 19% from the CMP, we maintain our BUY rating on the stock.

ssga-insights.title: Eurozone signals caution on June rate hike
ssga-insights.description: PMI weakness and softer inflation reshape rate paths across Europe and Japan, as demand destruction and policy shifts drive caution among major central banks.

ssga-insights.title: Risk-on is back, higher volatility stocks rebound
ssga-insights.description: Equities have recovered their poise, with high-volatility stocks benefiting from a renewed risk-on rally, despite geopolitical and economic uncertainty.

ssga-insights.title: Global markets tilt toward AI
ssga-insights.description: AI-driven gains reshape markets, but rising concentration risks challenge diversification across equities and traditional assets.

ssga-insights.title: What’s driving the surge in global interest rates?
ssga-insights.description: We discuss how oil prices, US inflation, and geopolitical tensions are impacting rates, and look at rates under 3 key geopolitical scenarios.

ssga-insights.title: Equities back to overweight
ssga-insights.description: We modestly increase equity exposure, moving back to overweight, while raising both total portfolio risk and active risk positioning.

ssga-insights.title: Japan is back, and how!
ssga-insights.description: Japan’s revival is gaining traction as political stability, firmer inflation, and semiconductor-led investment support a more durable growth regime.

ssga-insights.title: Equity duration matters, but earnings decide
ssga-insights.description: US equities outperform as resilient earnings offset headwinds, while valuation and FX pressures weigh on ex‑US markets.

ssga-insights.title: US inflation surprises but signals unclear
ssga-insights.description: Hot inflation, resilient UK growth, and Japan’s tightening path shape the outlook as markets weigh data noise against rising rates and geopolitical risks.

ssga-insights.title: Systematic High Quality Corporate Fixed Income: Q1 2026 commentary
ssga-insights.description: In a period marked by volatility, SAFI’s disciplined, systematic signals (value and momentum) helped deliver resilient performance and consistent alpha.

ssga-insights.title: Why Canada matters for developed ex-US equity allocation
ssga-insights.description: Investors using MSCI EAFE Index for their international allocation are missing out on exposure to Canada, the world’s ninth largest economy. Here’s why MSCI World ex USA may be a better option.

ssga-insights.title: Emerging Market Debt Commentary: April 2026
ssga-insights.description: EM debt rebounded in April as improving risk sentiment lifted both hard and local currency debt, though geopolitical tensions and oil kept markets cautious.

ssga-insights.title: How to determine your risk tolerance (and why it matters)
ssga-insights.description: What is risk tolerance in investing? Learn how to determine your risk tolerance, avoid panic selling, and build an investment approach you can stick to over the long term.

ssga-insights.title: Actively managed ETFs: A new chapter for global investors
ssga-insights.description: Actively managed ETFs are gaining rapid global adoption as investors seek active insight with the efficiency and flexibility of the ETF wrapper. Get the details.

ssga-insights.title: US labor data improve but risks remain ahead
ssga-insights.description: US labor data surprised to the upside last week, but mixed signals remain. Canada’s labor market stayed weak, while Australia signaled a pause before further tightening.

ssga-insights.title: Market pricing of UK growth is too optimistic
ssga-insights.description: Slowing migration, an ageing workforce and weak productivity threaten UK growth, reshaping fiscal dynamics and altering the outlook for gilts, equities and sterling.

ssga-insights.title: Tokenized money market funds (MMFs): Revolutionizing liquidity management
ssga-insights.description: Tokenized money market funds are changing how institutions move cash—helping to improve liquidity mobility, reducing friction, and making collateral more usable.

ssga-insights.title: Markets shift to selectivity phase
ssga-insights.description: Strong earnings persist, but markets are turning more selective, rewarding durable cash flows and penalizing crowded leadership.

ssga-insights.title: Tactical shift favors USD
ssga-insights.description: Geopolitical risk dominated April FX, with Iran tensions and oil volatility. We turned tactically positive on the USD amid resilient US growth and uncertainty.

ssga-insights.title: What is sector investing?
ssga-insights.description: Discover sector investing—and how it can help investors express their market views and target trends to capture upside potential without putting too much into any one stock.

ssga-insights.title: Monthly Cash Review – USD
ssga-insights.description: April calmed after March, but oil swings kept volatility alive. The Fed stayed on hold and signaled higher-for-longer; we modestly extended to lock in yield.

ssga-insights.title: Risk-on ETF flows in a river of hills market
ssga-insights.description: Markets surged in April amid volatility, with global equities and ETFs seeing strong flows as risk assets climbed despite uncertain headlines.

ssga-insights.title: What is the rising gold/copper price ratio signaling for investors?
ssga-insights.description: Today’s rising gold/copper ratio is less about growth fears—and more about mounting concerns over debt, debasement, and the global monetary order.

ssga-insights.title: What has happened to the great rotation?
ssga-insights.description: Geopolitical strife has led a pause in the “great rotation”, with US stocks rebounding. Profits are robust but leadership remains narrow and risks are elevated.

ssga-insights.title: Central banks hold, but tensions drive risk
ssga-insights.description: Central banks are holding steady, but rising geopolitical risks and stubborn inflation are pushing policymakers closer to renewed rate hikes across major economies.

ssga-insights.title: Raising the wealth management bar for Gen X women
ssga-insights.description: Despite making remarkable economic strides, Gen X women feel vastly underserved. And they present advisors with the opportunity to grow their business while helping to close the gender wealth gap.

ssga-insights.title: Internal succession planning: 5 key steps
ssga-insights.description: Many retiring financial advisors plan to pass the baton to an internal successor. Learn best practices to develop talent and execute a seamless transition.

ssga-insights.title: Iran war: Risk on, energy off
ssga-insights.description: Markets cheered the ceasefire, but the energy shock persists. Stagflation looks unlikely, yet oil shortages are straining economies and driving asset divergence.

ssga-insights.description: In times of crisis, DC plan participants are looking to their employers or plan sponsors for leadership, outreach and guidance.
